goalTitle: v19 Goal Runbook + Next Action Control Center

tasks.title: Contracts, fixtures, validators
acceptance: goal-runbook.v1, goal-next-action.v1, goal-prompt-pack.v1, and goal-closeout-report.v1 contracts validate known-good fixtures.
Invalid fixtures reject unsafe refs, duplicate task ids, empty acceptance, unknown roles, unknown event types, and prompt registration drift.

tasks.title: Managed goal runbook registry and symphony goal init
acceptance: Goal runbooks are stored in the managed registry only after dry-run and hash confirmation.
symphony goal init validates goal-runbook.v1 before writing managed state.

tasks.title: Next-action resolver
acceptance: goal-next-action.v1 resolves worker, reviewer, main-verifier, and release-manager next actions from explicit runbook and event evidence.
The resolver does not infer progress from branch names, file names, task titles, command text, or path text.

tasks.title: Goal prompt pack generator
acceptance: symphony goal prompt renders copy-only prompts for worker, reviewer, main-verifier, and release-manager roles.
Prompt generation does not execute prompts, invoke models, register events, or replace reviewer approval.
copyOnlyCommands: pnpm symphony goal prompt --goal v19-fixture --task task-1 --role worker --markdown

tasks.title: CLI integration for goal next, goal prompt, goal closeout, and symphony next
acceptance: symphony goal next, symphony goal closeout, and symphony next return stable v19 contract output.
CLI output remains copy-only and does not execute generated prompts or release gates.

tasks.title: Workbench Active Goal Control Center
acceptance: Workbench displays active goal runbook, next action, prompt preview, and closeout gaps through read-only GET routes.
Workbench does not execute prompts, expose confirm buttons, invoke shell/model actions, download artifacts, or infer evidence from prompt text.

tasks.title: Docs, operator guide, and contract index
acceptance: README, product contracts, Workbench operator guide, task evidence index, and release evidence describe v19 without claiming a release tag.
Docs keep release-ready tied to explicit event state and do not treat local command passes as release readiness.

tasks.title: Release verification, final closure, and tag evidence
acceptance: Release gates are run and recorded with exact command results.
Final closure records task evidence, release gate state, tag evidence state, and remaining blockers without fabricating tag or GitHub release evidence.
